title: A Man.
content:
Fate slew him, but he did not drop;
    She felled -- he did not fall --
    Impaled him on her fiercest stakes --
    He neutralized them all.

    She stung him, sapped his firm advance,
    But, when her worst was done,
    And he, unmoved, regarded her,
    Acknowledged him a man.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "A Man.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
